channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How President Trump is reshaping transgender people’s rights
item.description: Coming up at 9 a.m. on Tuesday, MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests break down President Donald Trump’s series of executive orders that significantly reshape transgender rights in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Nikhil Kumaran, MPR News

item.title: Nevada lawmakers target 'resort fee'-style rent practices, age for gun ownership
item.description: Hidden fees are so out-of-hand that it now costs money just to pay the rent. And it's a good bet that tenants aren't told that until they have signed the lease.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE access of recipient information: AP sources
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting commissioner has stepped down from her role at the agency over Department of Government Efficiency requests to access Social Security recipient information.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrat reintroduces 'fake electors' bill following governor's veto
item.description: Nevada lawmakers will once again debate a bill to penalize a person who creates a false slate of presidential electors -- except this time with lesser penalties.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk's DOGE seeks access to taxpayer data at IRS: AP sources
item.description: Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency is seeking access to troves of sensitive taxpayer data at the IRS.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio meets Saudi Arabian officials ahead of US-Russia talks expected to lay groundwork for Trump-Putin summit
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman and Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan al-Saud in Ridyah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NAACP urges Black consumers to steer buying power away from companies that have ditched DEI
item.description: The NAACP is pointing to several companies that it is urging black consumers to stay away from because they have rescinded their commitments to diversity, equity and inclusion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk says millions in Social Security database are between ages of 100 and 159
item.description: Elon Musk indicated that a Social Security database lists millions of centenarians "with the death field set to FALSE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a fatal midair collision near Washington.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrats grapple for response on education as GOP charges ahead
item.description: The Democratic Party is having to take a hard look at its education strategy as Republicans move aggressively on the issue with satisfaction in public education at its lowest point in decades.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration cuts hit FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: Nearly half of the FDA’s $6.9 billion budget comes from fees paid by companies the agency regulates, including drug and medical device makers, which allows the agency to hire extra scientists to swiftly review products. Eliminating those positions will not reduce government spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says 14th Amendment is for former slaves, not migrants
item.description: On Thursday, a fourth federal judge blocked the executive order issued by the president on his first day in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Activist Curtis Sliwa joins race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City mayoral race just got even more crowded. The founder of the Guardian Angels nonprofit, Curtis Sliwa, has officially filed paperwork to run for the city's top job. He also ran for mayor back in 2021 and lost to the current Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: House GOP budget resolution in jeopardy amid concerns from moderates
item.description: The House GOP’s budget resolution could be in jeopardy of not clearing the full chamber amid concerns from moderates over likely cuts to social safety net programs — particularly Medicaid — with a handful undecided on whether they will support the key measure. Reps. David Valadao (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: How Presidents Day has evolved from reverence to retail
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Like the other Founding Fathers, George Washington was uneasy about the idea of publicly celebrating his life. He was the first leader of a new republic, not a king. And yet the United States will once again commemorate its first president on Monday, 293 years after he was born.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: NYC funding for migrant hotels should go to Helene victims: NC sheriff
item.description: FEMA has taken more than $80 million in total from New York City, escalating a dispute between Trump and NYC.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump officials signal potential changes at NOAA, the weather and climate agency
item.description: Federal workers at the home agency for the National Weather Service are concerned about a potential overhaul by Trump officials focused on cutting government costs.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda, Michael Copley, and Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Calls for Mayor Adams’ resignation mount amid turmoil at DOJ
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Calls for Mayor Eric Adams’ resignation are mounting after the Department of Justice ordered his case to be dropped. One call came from Lt. Gov. Antonio Delgado, second-in-command to Gov. Kathy Hochul. Hochul is the only person with the power to remove Adams.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Immigration poll shows growing support for restrictions, but deep divisions remain
item.description: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows growing support for some restrictions on immigration. Still, many elements of President Trump's sweeping crackdown are unpopular with Democrats and independents.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Concerns over mass deportations lead to ‘Know Your Rights‘ discussion in Brooklyn Center
item.description: Amid concerns over the Trump Administration’s plans for mass deportations, nearly three dozen people who live in Brooklyn Park and Brooklyn Center attended a panel discussion Wednesday night to ask questions about how to respond if ICE shows up at a home or workplace, and also asked about the role o
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Bad landlords target of bill that provides tools to help Las Vegas tenants
item.description: Nevada cities and counties don't have a lot of options when a landlord ignores poor conditions that are putting tenants in danger. Lawmakers could use AB211 to change that.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Egg prices unite Nevada lawmakers as AB171 makes its way toward approval
item.description: Nevada lawmakers continued Tuesday to fast-track legislation that could bring down the price of eggs, pushing Assembly Bill 171 (AB171) closer to law.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
